ground_truth.title: Homelab projects to try this weekend (December 19 - 21)
ground_truth.author: Patrick Campanale
ground_truth.main_content: Are you ready to spin up some core services that every homelab should have? The three projects I'm showcasing this weekend all focus on core services in my own homelab, so here's why you should run them too. Home Assistant is a fully self-hosted local control platform that allows you to send commands to devices locally on your network instead of sending commands through remote servers. Paperless-ngx is a simple Docker container that handles organizing and indexing your scanned documents, with OCR technology making uploaded documents searchable. BookStack is a wiki designed to be 100% self-hosted, perfect for documenting your homelab setup so you don't have to redo research when deploying services again.
with: Are you ready to spin up some core services that every homelab should have?
Home Assistant allows you to do quite a few things within your smart home, including sending commands to devices locally on your network instead of through remote servers.
With paperless-ngx, you just scan documents to your computer and then upload them to the paperless server—it handles everything else from there.
BookStack is basically a wiki for your homelab, designed to be 100% self-hosted.
Just a few months ago, I was sitting at about 20 Docker containers running on my main server. Now, I'm up to 50, and I don't see it slowing down anytime soon.
